frictions.description: The `mission list` command always filters by the configured agent_id. Newly created tasks have agentId=null until claimed, so they do NOT appear in `mission list` output. This means the natural workflow of create→list does not work as expected — users must claim tasks before they appear in the default list view. This is a design choice rather than a bug, but may confuse users.
frictions.resolution: Worked around by claiming a task before testing list filters. The assertion specifies 'displays tasks for configured agent_id' which is technically correct behavior.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. VAL-CLI-003 (create task) works with and without --description flag. VAL-CLI-004 (list tasks) shows formatted table, filters by project and status work correctly, output matches API data. VAL-CLI-011 (list projects) shows all projects with IDs and names, matches API data. Note: list command filters by agent_id, so unclaimed tasks don't appear in default list.
